vertical: Data transformation SaaS — open-source dbt Core + dbt Cloud, ~300 employees, Series D
campaign_name: Warehouse-Native MAP Adoption — Data Team as Champion
objective: Convert dbt Labs from a MAP-adjacent prospect to a Conversion platform trial. Their data team already lives in the warehouse — they have Snowflake, BigQuery, and dbt natively. The gap is their marketing team is almost certainly running HubSpot or Marketo on top of that warehouse without a native connection. The champion is their Marketing Data Engineer (active Greenhouse posting) — the person hired to paper over that exact gap.

segment.name: dbt Labs — Marketing Data Engineer Champion + VP Marketing Economic Buyer
salesforce_filters: Contact.Title LIKE '%Marketing Data Engineer%' OR Contact.Title LIKE '%VP Marketing%' OR Contact.Title LIKE '%VP of Marketing%'
warehouse_traits: github_org = 'dbt-labs' AND repo_last_pushed_at >= DATEADD(day, -7, CURRENT_DATE) AND repo_name IN ('dbt-mcp', 'dbt-duckdb')
greenhouse_job_posting_active = TRUE AND greenhouse_job_title ILIKE '%marketing data engineer%' AND greenhouse_job_description ILIKE '%dbt%' AND greenhouse_job_description ILIKE '%Snowflake%'
intent_topic IN ('marketing attribution', 'warehouse-native analytics', 'CDP replacement', 'reverse ETL') AND intent_score >= 65
segment.estimated_size: 2–4 contacts (Marketing Data Engineer + VP Marketing as primary; up to 2 secondary data team contacts identified via GitHub org membership)

touches.timing: Day 0, 09:00 recipient local time, Tuesday or Wednesday send only
touches.subject: dbt-mcp + your HubSpot gap — a direct connection
touches.body_brief: Open by referencing the dbt-mcp repo push from {{github_repo_pushed_days_ago}} days ago as proof of dbt Labs' agent-native orientation. Transition to the MAP ceiling observation: the Greenhouse JD for {{greenhouse_job_title}} explicitly asks for someone to build attribution 'outside of HubSpot' — which is the exact sentence Conversion was built to delete. Explain in 3 sentences: Conversion's execution, QA, and optimization agents run directly on top of {{prospect_warehouse_stack}} with no ETL hop; segmentation is written in SQL the new hire already knows; no HubSpot sync required. Single CTA: 'See a 60-second Snowflake-native segment built live — reply to book.' Keep under 180 words. No decks, no PDF attachments.
qa_rules: Suppress if Contact.Email_Opt_Out__c = TRUE or Contact.Unsubscribed__c = TRUE before send
Verify {{github_repo_pushed_days_ago}} resolves to integer ≤ 14; if NULL, suppress touch and flag for manual review
Verify {{greenhouse_job_title}} token resolves to non-empty string; if NULL substitute 'Marketing Data Engineer' and log substitution
Spam-gate: subject line must not contain ALL CAPS words; flag if spam score (SpamAssassin) > 3.0
Deduplicate: if both Marketing Data Engineer and VP Marketing contacts are in segment, send step 1 to Marketing Data Engineer only; VP Marketing enters at step 3
Confirm sending domain has valid DKIM + DMARC pass before queue commit
touches.success_metric: Email open rate ≥ 45% (persona: technically literate, low volume outbound); reply rate ≥ 8% within 72 hours

touches.timing: Day 1 — fires if contact visits getconversion.com/warehouse-native within 48 hours of step 1 send; else fires Day 3 on next session
touches.subject: Your dbt models are already your segments
touches.body_brief: Short contextual banner (≤ 40 words) shown on Conversion's warehouse-native product page. Message: 'You already wrote the segment in dbt — Conversion just runs it. See {{prospect_warehouse_stack}} → campaign in under 5 minutes.' CTA button: 'Start free warehouse trial'. Banner should NOT fire on pricing page to avoid premature price anchoring.
qa_rules: Banner must not render on /pricing, /about, or /blog URL paths — enforce URL suppression list in banner targeting rules
Fire only once per contact per session; suppress repeat impressions within same 24-hour window
Verify {{prospect_warehouse_stack}} resolves before render; default to 'your data warehouse' if NULL to prevent broken token display
A/B variant selection is owned by optimization agent — execution agent must not override variant assignment
Confirm contact cookie/identity resolution matches CRM contact ID before personalization tokens are injected
touches.success_metric: Banner CTR ≥ 12%; trial sign-up page scroll depth ≥ 60% among click-throughs

touches.timing: Day 2, assigned to AE by 08:00 AE local time; task due same day EOD
touches.subject: AE Outreach — dbt Labs dual-thread: Marketing Data Engineer + VP Marketing (Greenhouse + GitHub signals live)
touches.body_brief: AE task brief with two sub-tasks: (A) LinkedIn connect + voice note to VP Marketing — reference the conscious decision to hire someone to build attribution outside HubSpot as a strategic bet, and offer a 20-min exec framing of what warehouse-native MAP means for their roadmap. (B) Direct email to Marketing Data Engineer (or hiring manager if role not yet filled) — lead with the dbt-mcp and dbt-duckdb repos as shared context, offer to co-build a live Snowflake segment using their actual dbt schema during a screen-share. AE note: buying window is <21 days per intent score decay model — do not batch this task. Include Salesforce task fields: Task.Subject, Task.Priority = 'High', Task.ActivityDate = today, Task.WhoId = both contact IDs, Task.Description = full brief text.
qa_rules: Task must write back to Salesforce Task object with Task.Priority__c = 'High' and Task.Campaign_Source__c = 'Warehouse-Native MAP Adoption'
Verify AE assignment: Account.OwnerId must resolve to an active Salesforce user; if unassigned, escalate to SDR manager queue
Confirm both contact IDs (Marketing Data Engineer, VP Marketing) are present in Task.WhoId before task creation; if only one contact exists, create single-threaded task and flag for enrichment
Do not create duplicate tasks: check for existing open Task.Subject LIKE '%dbt Labs%' created in last 7 days before writing new task
Task description must not contain unparsed tokens — validate all {{tokens}} resolve before Salesforce write
touches.success_metric: AE task completed (Task.Status = 'Completed') within 24 hours; LinkedIn connection accepted OR email reply from either champion within 72 hours of task creation

touches.timing: Day 5, 08:30 recipient local time — send only if no reply received to step 1 and AE task not marked 'Completed — Replied'
touches.subject: The attribution model your new hire will have to rebuild (unless…)
touches.body_brief: Nurture email targeting Marketing Data Engineer persona (or VP Marketing if MDE contact not yet resolved). Open with a concrete technical narrative: 'The first 90 days of a Marketing Data Engineer at a HubSpot shop are usually the same — export CSVs, rebuild attribution in dbt, try to push segments back into HubSpot via reverse ETL, watch it break on contact ID mismatches.' Pivot: Conversion's warehouse sync means that 90-day rebuild is a 90-minute setup. Offer a specific artifact — a public dbt package or SQL template that maps directly to {{prospect_warehouse_stack}} schema — as the trial entry point. CTA: 'Grab the dbt attribution starter pack → start your trial.' Include one-sentence social proof from a data-forward customer (anonymized vertical match). Under 220 words.
qa_rules: Suppress if step 1 generated a reply OR if AE task is marked Task.Status = 'Completed — Replied' in Salesforce — do not double-touch an engaged contact
Suppress if Contact.Unsubscribed__c = TRUE or bounce recorded on step 1
Verify {{similar_customer_vertical}} token resolves to a non-empty, non-competitor reference; if NULL, remove social proof sentence entirely rather than render broken
Subject line A/B variant (optimization agent owns selection): variant B = 'What the first 90 days of your new Marketing Data Engineer look like' — do not send both variants to same contact
Confirm send-time suppression window: do not send between Friday 17:00 and Monday 07:00 recipient local time
touches.success_metric: Email open rate ≥ 38%; dbt attribution starter pack download OR trial sign-up click ≥ 10% of opens

touches.timing: Day 7, fire if no trial sign-up by Day 6 EOD; send as LinkedIn InMail to VP Marketing contact
touches.subject: The hire you just approved already knows how to use this
touches.body_brief: LinkedIn InMail to VP Marketing. Short and exec-toned (≤ 120 words). Lead: 'You approved a {{greenhouse_job_title}} hire specifically to get past {{account.current_map}}'s limits. Conversion is what that person will reach for once they're in seat — warehouse-native MAP that runs on {{prospect_warehouse_stack}} without any sync layer.' Offer: 'Happy to give you a 15-min preview so you're not in a cold eval when they start.' Do not attach decks. No feature lists. One CTA: calendar link or reply. Reference dbt-mcp signal lightly as evidence of technical culture alignment: 'We saw your team ship dbt-mcp — the same agent-native design philosophy is in our platform core.'
qa_rules: Confirm LinkedIn contact profile URL is populated on Contact.LinkedIn_URL__c before InMail dispatch; if NULL, skip step 5 and log for enrichment — do not send to generic LinkedIn search result
Suppress if VP Marketing contact has already replied to any prior touch in this sequence (check Contact.Last_Reply_Date__c against campaign enrollment date)
InMail character count must not exceed 300 characters including subject; QA agent must validate length before send
Verify {{ae.calendar_link}} is a valid URL (regex check); if broken, substitute ae.email CTA instead
Do not send InMail within 48 hours of a prior LinkedIn touch from AE (step 3 sub-task B) — check Task.ActivityDate on AE task before dispatch
touches.success_metric: InMail open rate ≥ 55%; calendar booking OR positive reply rate ≥ 15% within 5 days of send

touches.timing: Day 10, 08:00 AE local time — fires unconditionally as sequence close/escalation task regardless of prior engagement
touches.subject: AE Escalation — dbt Labs trial close or disqualify by Day 21 (intent decay window)
touches.body_brief: Final AE escalation task. Intent score decay model gives a ≤ 21-day window from first GitHub signal (Day 0 = dbt-mcp push). By Day 10 the window is 55% elapsed. Task instructs AE to: (1) Review full engagement log — opens, clicks, replies, LinkedIn accepts — surfaced in Conversion dashboard. (2) If any positive signal: call both champions same day, use trial-close script anchored on the dbt attribution starter pack from step 4. (3) If zero engagement: disqualify contact from active sequence, move Account.Lifecycle_Stage__c to 'Nurture — Dark', schedule re-signal check in 45 days via Salesforce follow-up task. (4) Log outcome in Task.Description with one of three dispositions: 'Trial Started', 'Meeting Booked', 'Disqualified — Dark'. AE must not leave task open past Day 21.
qa_rules: Task must not be created if Account.Conversion_Trial_Active__c = TRUE (trial already started — escalation is moot)

{{campaign_engagement_summary}} token must pull from Conversion engagement log API and resolve to a non-empty string summarizing opens/clicks/replies; if API call fails, substitute 'Review Conversion dashboard manually' and alert MarOps
{{intent_score_decay_date}} must resolve to a valid date in ISO 8601 format; if NULL, default to enrollment_date + 21 days and log assumption
Verify Task.WhoId resolves to at least one active contact before Salesforce write
Task.Priority__c must be set to 'High'; Task.Campaign_Source__c = 'Warehouse-Native MAP Adoption'; Task.Type = 'Escalation Close'
touches.success_metric: AE task disposition logged within 24 hours of creation; trial start rate OR meeting booked rate ≥ 30% of accounts reaching step 6

optimization_triggers.condition: Contact replies to step 1 email within 72 hours OR clicks trial CTA in step 2 banner
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent fast-tracks contact to step 3 AE task immediately (skip Day 2 delay); suppress steps 4 and 5 to prevent over-touching an engaged champion; re-segment contact into 'Hot — Active Trial Pursuit' cohort; AE task priority escalated to Task.Priority__c = 'Critical'

optimization_triggers.condition: github_org = 'dbt-labs' AND repo_last_pushed_at refreshes with a NEW repo push (any repo) after campaign enrollment and within campaign runtime window
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent injects updated {{github_repo_name}} and {{github_repo_pushed_days_ago}} tokens into all queued touches; re-scores intent signal; if intent_score increases by ≥ 10 points, compress remaining touch cadence by 48 hours across all pending steps

optimization_triggers.condition: greenhouse_job_posting_active flips to FALSE (role filled or pulled) AND no trial started
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent pivots champion targeting: deprioritize Marketing Data Engineer outreach, escalate VP Marketing thread (steps 5 and 6) immediately; update AE task brief to note 'MDE role filled — new hire may be in seat, direct AE outreach to confirm identity and re-thread'; re-enrich Contact.Title__c via enrichment queue

optimization_triggers.condition: intent_score drops below 50 before Day 10 (signal decay without engagement)
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent pauses steps 4–6, moves account to 'Nurture — Cooling' cohort, schedules a re-entry check at Day 30 conditional on new intent signal ≥ 60 or new GitHub push; logs pause reason in Account.Campaign_Pause_Reason__c

optimization_triggers.condition: VP Marketing contact accepts AE LinkedIn connect (step 3 sub-task or step 5) AND no trial started
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent immediately generates a personalized AE call brief using live engagement data and injects into open AE task description; suppress remaining email and in_app_banner touches to VP Marketing to preserve channel for AE-owned conversation; set Contact.Preferred_Channel__c = 'AE Direct'

optimization_triggers.condition: Account.Conversion_Trial_Active__c flips to TRUE at any point during campaign
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent immediately terminates all queued touches (email, in_app_banner, linkedin, sms) for all contacts in segment; cancels open AE prospecting tasks and replaces with onboarding task; transitions account lifecycle stage to 'Trial — Active Onboarding'; fires success event to Salesforce Opportunity stage update: StageName = 'Trial Started'

optimization_triggers.condition: Conference signal detected: any contact in segment registers for or is confirmed attending dbt Coalesce, DataCouncil, or MADTechSummit within campaign runtime window (sourced from event_registration warehouse trait)
optimization_triggers.action: Optimization agent triggers conference_meet workflow: creates high-priority AE task to request in-person or virtual meeting at event venue (Task.Subject = 'Conference Meet Request — dbt Labs @ {{event_name}}'); injects {{event_name}} and {{event_date}} tokens into a bespoke step 5 LinkedIn InMail variant; compresses remaining touch cadence to conclude before event date minus 3 days to ensure AE conversation happens pre-event

pipeline_projection.expected_renewals: N/A — net-new trial conversion target. Expected outcome: 1 trial start within 21 days. Trial-to-paid conversion assumed at 35% (data-native ICP benchmark) = 0.35 net-new logo. ACV estimate: $48,000–$72,000 (mid-market warehouse-native tier, 2–5 seat marketing team + warehouse connector). Pipeline contribution: $48K–$72K ARR per won logo.
pipeline_projection.ae_efficiency: AE touch count capped at 2 structured tasks (steps 3 and 6) plus opportunistic LinkedIn (step 3 sub-task). Estimated AE time investment: 45–60 minutes total across 21-day window. Full engagement signal context surfaced in Conversion dashboard eliminates cold-call prep time. Dual-thread (MDE + VP Marketing) increases meeting rate without doubling AE effort due to shared task structure.
pipeline_projection.campaign_runtime: 21 days from enrollment (Day 0 = campaign trigger date, anchored to dbt-mcp GitHub push signal 3 days ago, effective window expires in ≤ 18 days from brief submission). Hard stop at Day 21 — intent decay model predicts signal half-life at 21 days for GitHub-triggered outbound. AE must log disposition by Day 21 or account auto-moves to 'Nurture — Dark'.
pipeline_projection.downside: Downside scenario (probability: ~35%): Marketing Data Engineer role is filled externally before campaign closes; new hire arrives with a pre-existing MAP vendor preference (e.g., Census, Hightouch, or Segment) established in prior role. VP Marketing defers MAP decision to new hire's 30/60/90 ramp plan, pushing eval window to Q2+. In this scenario: trial conversion probability drops to ~12%; pipeline contribution compresses to $0 in current quarter. Mitigation: step 3 AE task includes explicit instruction to ask VP Marketing for intro to new hire on Day 1 of their start; optimization trigger 3 catches role-filled signal and pivots outreach accordingly. Secondary downside: dbt Labs is acquired or announces a strategic partnership with an existing MAP vendor during campaign window (low probability, <5%), which would trigger immediate disqualification and Closed Lost logging.

meta.campaign_name: Warehouse-Native MAP Adoption — Data Team as Champion
meta.objective: Convert dbt Labs from a MAP-adjacent prospect to a Conversion platform trial. dbt Labs' data team already operates natively in Snowflake and BigQuery with dbt at the core. The marketing stack gap is acute: their Greenhouse JD for a Marketing Data Engineer explicitly calls out 'building attribution from scratch outside of HubSpot', confirming the team has hit the ceiling of a warehouse-disconnected MAP. Active GitHub signals (dbt-mcp pushed 3d ago, dbt-duckdb pushed 6d ago) confirm an agent-native, embedded-warehouse orientation that maps directly to Conversion's architecture. Two champions are targeted: (1) the incoming/current Marketing Data Engineer — the person hired to solve the exact problem Conversion eliminates, and (2) the VP Marketing who approved the hire and owns the budget. The buying window is live and compressing — campaign must close to trial within 21 days.

buying_signals.signal: Marketing Data Engineer role on Greenhouse requires dbt, Snowflake, and attribution outside HubSpot
dbt-labs/dbt-mcp pushed 3 days ago — active investment in agent-native tooling
dbt-labs/dbt-duckdb active — embedded/local warehouse execution interest

signals.description: Marketing Data Engineer role posted on Greenhouse — JD requires dbt, Snowflake, and 'building attribution from scratch outside of HubSpot'. Direct MAP ceiling signal.
dbt-labs/dbt-mcp repo pushed 3 days ago — they're building an MCP server for dbt, signaling active investment in agent-native tooling. Conversion's agent model is a natural fit.
dbt-labs/dbt-duckdb repo active — DuckDB integration signals interest in embedded/local warehouse execution, consistent with warehouse-native MAP interest.

why_now.rationale: 3 signals detected in the last 6 days. Buying window closes in ~8 days.
